posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 6 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1234 others

Bo Nickal vs Kyle Daukaus
Pick: Nickal via decision
Nickal is -345 on Draftkings and -390 on Fanduel
75% of Tapology users are picking Nickal (79% decision)
Nickal is 2.9 years younger (30.4 vs 33.3)
Both fighters have 76” reach
Nickal has +1.28 strike differential/min vs +0.42
Daukaus has +2.11 strike differential/min @distance vs +0.41
Daukaus proj. +6.2 significant strikes attempted
Nickal proj. +6.9 significant strikes landed
Nickal proj. +3.1 head strikes landed
Nickal has 45% head strike accuracy, 75% head strike defense
Daukaus has 43% head strike accuracy, 53% head strike defense
Nickal has 2 pt edge in HSA, 22 pt edge in HSD
Nickal proj. 0.68 knockdowns vs 0.36
Nickal proj. 2.1 takedowns landed (5.6 attempts) vs 1.0 (8.3 attempts)
Nickal proj. 2.50 submission attempts vs 2.10
Nickal proj. 43.4% finish chance vs 37.4% and 19.2% decision
Good upside on Nickal via finish (via decision is safer)
Nickal will likely win the head strike battle and grappling

* Edited at 03.10.2026,  5:46 AM ET *

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 6 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1234 others

Diego Lopes vs Steve Garcia
Pick: Lopes via round 2 KO
Diego Lopes is -198 on Draftkings, -210 on Fanduel, -185 on BetOnline
77% of Tapology users (676 users) are picking Lopes (62% KO)
Lopes is 2.6 years younger (31.5 vs 34.1)
Garcia has 3” reach edge (75” vs 72”)
Garcia has +3.27 strike differential/min vs -0.73
Garcia has +4.13 strike differential/min @distance vs -1.37
Garcia proj. +44.8 significant strikes attempted
Garcia proj. +31.3 significant strikes landed
Garcia proj. +24.6 head strikes landed
Garcia has 41% head strike accuracy, 66% head strike defense
Lopes has 44% head strike accuracy, 51% head strike defense
Lopes has 3 pt edge in HSA, Garcia has 15 pt edge in HSD
Lopes proj. 1.05 knockdowns vs 0.98
Garcia has 2.6% knockdown rate, 3.7% knocked down rate (knocked down early in career)
Lopes has 1.1% knockdown rate, 0.0% knocked down rate
Lopes proj. 1.40 submission attempts vs 0.40
Lopes proj. 37.0% finish chance vs 34.1% and 28.9% decision
Lopes via KO is obviously the safe play based on the betting odds, user decisions
Garcia is obviously a good underdog play
I recommend hedging by taking Lopes via KO on here and betting Garcia
I may even take Garcia on here because he is projected to win the striking by so much
Garcia has 15:1 KO ratio (only time KO’d was in round 1 vs Maheshate
8/15 KO’s for Garcia were in round 1
6/15 KO’s for Garcia were in round 2
6/11 KO’s for Lopes were in round 1
4/11 KO’s for Lopes were in round 2
Lopes does have 12:0 submission win/loss ratio vs 0:1 for Garcia
Only 11% of users are picking submission so Lopes via KO is safer is you like him

I dont think name values is the issue with the card. I’m a big believer in quality of the fights over name value. I rather see current stars over popular stars from yesteryear who’s been retired for years or hasn’t had a good performance in a long time

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 6 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1233 others

I mean:
Topuria vs Gaethje - the only fight that makes sense for Ilia now, as Islam at 170 would be undeserved, and for everyone saying Arman, Gaethje is literally the interim champ plus he's American so it makes sense for this card (Arman has to be next)

Pereira vs Gane - should be a great striking matchup (yeah I know it should've been Pereira vs Jones but I personally don't want to see Jones anywhere near the octagon ever again)

O'Malley vs Zahabi - kinda weird matchmaking, it probably should've been O'Malley vs Sandhagen or Umar

Ruffy vs Chandler - even though it's another undeserved fight up the rankings for Chandler, I don't mind this fight as long as he gets knocked the f out

Bo vs Daukaus - why make this fight, and why put either of these guys on the White House card? I guess if it has to be Bo, why isn't it against Colby?

Lopes vs Garcia - lowkey a banger fight, but would've prefered to see it as a main of Noche UFC or some other fight night

* Edited at 03.17.2026,  2:14 AM ET *

posts.author: Xion
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 6 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1233 others

Topuria vs. Gaethje
Ilia Topuria, TKO, R1
folk😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭Naw deadass though Gaethje is ******* ******. I love him but come on i feel like he cant even fluke a win here. Bluds getting slimed out on the south lawn of the white house and theres nothing anyone can do about it unless he pulls out. Honestly i wish he would i'm a massive Gaycheese fan hence the pfp and if Makhachev wasnt cowering away from this fight then Justin wouldnt have to be sacrificed. El Matador will slump Gaethje out cold in round one after a fireworks fun first round he'll catch Justin over the top of a punch before he's about to come in to start a combination similar to the way he KO'd Charles. I feel like face first is the way to go here. I mean its easy to imagine him going down face first because its already happened to him but i just imagine a right hook around the pipe ******* spalling his head back and he just falls forward onto his face. It'll be too sad to see him go down on his back and see his unconscious face. Ilia Topuria is the best pfp fighter in the world right now and there's just no way around it and i wish the Makhachev fight could just happen so we can see who is the greatest potentially of all time but its kinda silly to hold up the divisions for superfights yet again i guess. And if Islam doesnt have enough time to get on his new 170 cycle then it wont be competitive so theres that too😞

Pereira vs. Gane
Alex Pereira, TKO, R2
I like this fight even though it's kind of ******* stupid. STOP GIVING CYRIL GANE TITLE SHOTS HE'S NOT THE BEST IN THE DIVISION VOLKOV JUST BEAT HIS BUM ASS. Lost all his title fights due to mental ******ation. So basically we are getting Tom Aspinall vs Alex Pereira later this year. Unreal storyline there. They both fought on 295, realised greatness on the same night and ever since have been dominating, and now will finally meet to decide who is the best in the world. So ******* badass i cant wait. I dont see Cyril Gane winning this fight even though he is a tricky striker and has good movement and is very fast i dont see that style having massive success in this fight. Alex Pereira is one of the best strikers in UFC history and its a fact. He's also one of the fastest heavier weight class fighters maybe ever. Not to mention the "ungodly" power he possesses its just a bad matchup for Gane. Despite looking good in all his fights on the feet due to his hippity hoppity around the outside style, he isnt as good as he's made out to be. He's high level for heavyweight standards and that's about it. Slick striker but its very easy to break his rhythm and make his brain scramble. His best weapons are his kicks and his jab and thy are relatively easy to counter if you are a former glory kickboxing champion and hall of famer. We've seen Pereira deal with kicks before and as seen on the most recent Ankalaev fight, the forward pressure of Alex just nullifies the effect that kicks have on him. Gane is a cleaner striker and kicker than Big Ank but he leaves himself too open and tries to use his head movement as his defence and unless you're a fat slow lardass 265 pound truck driver, after a few minutes of reading him you can start to land clean on him and break his fragile defence. I think Alex is gonna pressure early and Gane is gonna try to stick and move and do what he usually does but Alex is gonna stay on him like the ******* savage he is and just chop the **** outta his leg. He's gonna force Gane to switch stances a lot and that will play into Pereira's game. When he gets kicked, he'll switch to southpaw and then when he's there Pereira will be popping his jab and using his distance management to bait Gane in and land his slip counter combinations. Then when Gane gets hit or pressured again he switches back and gets his leg slapped again. Eventually Gane is gonna misread the distance against the fence when Alex pressures him and at the end of a combination late R1 or 2, Cereal will try to circle out away from Pereira's power hand and get cracked with a left hook and ground and pounded into a bloody mess in front of Donald J. Trump himself. Aspinall vs Pereira co-main evented by Makhachev vs Topuria. Absolute Cinema.
